Package calendar.controller

Class HeadlessController

  • java.lang.Object
    • calendar.controller.HeadlessController
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    CalendarController
    public class HeadlessController
extends java.lang.Object
implements CalendarController
    Headless controller for the calendar application. Reads and executes commands from a file sequentially.

    This controller processes commands in batch mode:

    • Reads all commands from specified file
    • Executes each command in order
    • Does not require user interaction
    • Exits after processing all commands or encountering "exit"

    If the file does not end with an "exit" command, displays an error but still terminates gracefully.

      • HeadlessController

        public HeadlessController​(CalendarModel model,
                          CalendarView view,
                          java.lang.String inputFile)
        Creates a headless controller.
        Parameters:
        model - the calendar model
        view - the calendar view
        inputFile - path to the input file containing commands

    • Method Detail

      • processCommand

        public void processCommand​(java.lang.String command)
                    throws InvalidCommandException
        Description copied from interface: CalendarController
        Processes a single command string.
        Specified by:
        processCommand in interface CalendarController
        Parameters:
        command - the command to process
        Throws:
        InvalidCommandException - if the command is invalid or malformed

      • run

        public void run()
        Description copied from interface: CalendarController
        Runs the main application loop.

        Behavior depends on implementation:

        • Interactive: Continuously reads and processes commands until "exit"
        • Headless: Processes all commands from input source, then exits
        Specified by:
        run in interface CalendarController
